items.correct: Removing material from a solid block to reveal the form (e.g., carving stone).
items.why: Students confuse 'subtractive' with arithmetic. In sculpture, additive = building up (clay, welding); subtractive = taking away (carving). Michelangelo described 'freeing' figures from the marble - quintessential subtractive thinking.

items.correct: Visual equilibrium achieved with different elements of equal visual weight on each side.
items.why: Students confuse asymmetry with imbalance. Asymmetrical balance is still BALANCE - achieved through unequal but visually weighted elements (e.g., a small dark shape balancing a large light one).

items.q: What is the difference between relief and intaglio printing?
items.correct: Relief prints from raised surfaces; intaglio prints from incised lines below the surface.
items.misconception: They are different names for the same process.
items.random_wrong: Relief is for paper, intaglio is for fabric.
items.why: Students don't grasp the inverse logic. Lino cuts AWAY non-printing areas (relief - raised inked); etching incises lines that HOLD ink below the surface (intaglio - ink pressed up into paper).

items.q: Angel of the North is located near:
items.why: It's Gateshead. “Brighton” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Gormley often starts a work using:
items.why: It's A body cast of himself. “A photograph of a model” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Wingspan of Angel of the North in metres?
items.why: Exact answer: 54. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
